text: Honestly this movie was mid. The CGI looked great but the plot had more holes than my socks. Would not recommend unless you have 2 hours to waste.

my manager approved my PTO and then scheduled a mandatory meeting for the same week. absolute classic.

hot take: most productivity hacks are just things normal people do automatically

the fact that this is controversial says more about the state of the debate than the idea itself

This is a game-changer for accessibility honestly. Finally someone got it right.
key takeaways from today's disaster: never deploy on a Friday, always have a rollback plan, coffee is not optional
thrilled to announce I got rejected from yet another job application. absolutely crushing it out here
feel free to disagree but remote work is just better for most knowledge workers and I won't be taking questions
In today's rapidly evolving field of watching TV, I've decided to watch more TV. Thought leadership content incoming.
the fact that we're still arguing about tabs vs spaces in 2026 is a testament to how much free time developers have
someone on my team just asked what a linked list is during a code review. I am so tired.
After 8 years at the same company I'm ready for something new. Excited to announce I'm joining a new team next month. Nervous but ready.
Lost a big client today. It stings. But I learned more from this failure than from most of my wins. Back to work.
Reminder that 'culture fit' is often just code for 'people who look and think like us'. Push back on it.
The interview process at most companies is broken. We test for trivia instead of judgment.
5 years ago I was rejected from this company. Today I got the offer. Persistence matters.
Unpopular take: your network matters more than your skills up to a certain level. Skills matter more after that.
The best managers I've had were the ones who got out of my way and let me do my job.
I gave a terrible presentation today. Froze up, lost my place, the whole thing. But I did it, and next time will be better.
Hiring managers: a gap in someone's resume is not a red flag. Life happens. Judge the person in front of you.
Just hit a milestone I set for myself 3 years ago. Going to sit with that feeling for a minute before setting the next one.
The startup I joined 18 months ago just shut down. Here's what I learned: cash flow beats growth metrics every time.
Not every career move is up. Sometimes lateral is the right call and that's fine.
Fired my first employee today. Worst day of my professional life. Getting it right matters even when it's hard.
In today's rapidly evolving job market, my advice is simple: be adaptable. That's it. That's the post.
I've been in this industry for 15 years. The bottom line is that most of what gets called innovation is just repackaging.
When it comes to hiring, I've seen companies pass on brilliant people because of a formatting choice on their CV. It's embarrassing.
Thrilled to announce I'm taking a month off. No consulting, no side projects, just nothing. Apparently this is controversial.

3 years of building something from nothing. It didn't work out. And that's okay. On to the next one.

the codebase is a testament to years of good intentions and bad deadlines. I say this with love.
